---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: f7f1bdf8ba0fa97f8b8feaecaf64dd1641549b82
findings:
  - { id: judged-ac4-real-root-bindings-proof-vacuity, kind: judged, text: "ac-3/ac-4's 'proven on the real root verdi.bindings.yaml, not only on a synthetic fixture' leg rests entirely on the implicit green pass of `verdi lint` inside `make lint-store`: every committed test (TestVL003_RootBindings_BadBareAC_Reds, _TypoFragmentAC_RedsByName, _AllCorrect_StaysClean) exercises synthetic temp-dir stores only, and nothing pins non-vacuity on the real repository — no assertion anywhere that BuildSnapshot at this repo's actual root decodes RootBindings non-nil and that the real file's fragment-qualified entries are genuinely cross-checked. Because snapshot.go maps an absent root file to (nil, nil) and checkBindings is then a silent no-op, a future regression in store.FindRoot resolution or the root-read path would evaporate the real-file proof silently while lint-store stays green and all synthetic tests keep passing — the exact vacuity shape the fixture-level tests (e.g. the ServicesOnlyLoop_Blind non-vacuity leg) guard against everywhere except on the one real file the spec says the proof must run on. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Real ratchet gap in the real-file proof (a root-resolution regression would evaporate it silently while synthetic tests stay green): SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave — a specalign-style pin that the real root bindings file is discovered and checkBindings fires on this repository." }
  - { id: judged-ac3-root-guard-path-equality, kind: judged, text: "checkBindings' rootDiscoveredAsService guard (vl003.go) decides 'a Service is already rooted at the module root' by string equality svc.Dir == in.Root, but DiscoverServices absolutizes/cleans Dir via filepath.Abs while engine.Run passes the caller's root string through verbatim — a library or test caller handing Run a relative, trailing-slash, or unclean root makes the guard never fire, so in exactly the hypothetical store the guard exists for (a .flowmap.yaml at the module root) the one root bindings file would be validated twice under two labels ('verdi.bindings.yaml (\u003cservice\u003e)' and 'verdi.bindings.yaml (root)'), duplicating every finding. Harmless for this repository per D6-4, but the guard's own documented intent is defeated by path-normalization asymmetry rather than by semantics. (confidence 0.25)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Path-normalization asymmetry defeats the guard's documented intent for library/test callers (harmless for this repo per D6-4): SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave — normalize in.Root before comparison." }
digest: sha256:17040954b0a5e26e6d3c15093212c5c16b2db119055b939d6a76ba91e0d9fbbc
integrity: sha256:898c76da9d40c4937e62d87c3e0704bc940d58c7785515038b5b3d7caacc6c62
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"ac4-real-root-bindings-proof-vacuity\",\"text\":\"ac-3/ac-4's 'proven on the real root verdi.bindings.yaml, not only on a synthetic fixture' leg rests entirely on the implicit green pass of `verdi lint` inside `make lint-store`: every committed test (TestVL003_RootBindings_BadBareAC_Reds, _TypoFragmentAC_RedsByName, _AllCorrect_StaysClean) exercises synthetic temp-dir stores only, and nothing pins non-vacuity on the real repository — no assertion anywhere that BuildSnapshot at this repo's actual root decodes RootBindings non-nil and that the real file's fragment-qualified entries are genuinely cross-checked. Because snapshot.go maps an absent root file to (nil, nil) and checkBindings is then a silent no-op, a future regression in store.FindRoot resolution or the root-read path would evaporate the real-file proof silently while lint-store stays green and all synthetic tests keep passing — the exact vacuity shape the fixture-level tests (e.g. the ServicesOnlyLoop_Blind non-vacuity leg) guard against everywhere except on the one real file the spec says the proof must run on.\",\"confidence\":0.45},{\"id\":\"ac3-root-guard-path-equality\",\"text\":\"checkBindings' rootDiscoveredAsService guard (vl003.go) decides 'a Service is already rooted at the module root' by string equality svc.Dir == in.Root, but DiscoverServices absolutizes/cleans Dir via filepath.Abs while engine.Run passes the caller's root string through verbatim — a library or test caller handing Run a relative, trailing-slash, or unclean root makes the guard never fire, so in exactly the hypothetical store the guard exists for (a .flowmap.yaml at the module root) the one root bindings file would be validated twice under two labels ('verdi.bindings.yaml (\u003cservice\u003e)' and 'verdi.bindings.yaml (root)'), duplicating every finding. Harmless for this repository per D6-4, but the guard's own documented intent is defeated by path-normalization asymmetry rather than by semantics.\",\"confidence\":0.25}]}" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-21, commit: f7f1bdf8ba0fa97f8b8feaecaf64dd1641549b82 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/ritual-traps@f7f1bdf8ba0fa97f8b8feaecaf64dd1641549b82, spec/ritual-traps@931e3b40be1d375d297b08128443c37e46c93bbd], digest: sha256:17040954b0a5e26e6d3c15093212c5c16b2db119055b939d6a76ba91e0d9fbbc, integrity: sha256:898c76da9d40c4937e62d87c3e0704bc940d58c7785515038b5b3d7caacc6c62, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-ac4-real-root-bindings-proof-vacuity** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3/ac-4's 'proven on the real root verdi.bindings.yaml, not only on a synthetic fixture' leg rests entirely on the implicit green pass of `verdi lint` inside `make lint-store`: every committed test (TestVL003_RootBindings_BadBareAC_Reds, _TypoFragmentAC_RedsByName, _AllCorrect_StaysClean) exercises synthetic temp-dir stores only, and nothing pins non-vacuity on the real repository — no assertion anywhere that BuildSnapshot at this repo's actual root decodes RootBindings non-nil and that the real file's fragment-qualified entries are genuinely cross-checked. Because snapshot.go maps an absent root file to (nil, nil) and checkBindings is then a silent no-op, a future regression in store.FindRoot resolution or the root-read path would evaporate the real-file proof silently while lint-store stays green and all synthetic tests keep passing — the exact vacuity shape the fixture-level tests (e.g. the ServicesOnlyLoop_Blind non-vacuity leg) guard against everywhere except on the one real file the spec says the proof must run on. (confidence 0.45) — Real ratchet gap in the real-file proof (a root-resolution regression would evaporate it silently while synthetic tests stay green): SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave — a specalign-style pin that the real root bindings file is discovered and checkBindings fires on this repository.
- **judged-ac3-root-guard-path-equality** [accepted-deviation]: checkBindings' rootDiscoveredAsService guard (vl003.go) decides 'a Service is already rooted at the module root' by string equality svc.Dir == in.Root, but DiscoverServices absolutizes/cleans Dir via filepath.Abs while engine.Run passes the caller's root string through verbatim — a library or test caller handing Run a relative, trailing-slash, or unclean root makes the guard never fire, so in exactly the hypothetical store the guard exists for (a .flowmap.yaml at the module root) the one root bindings file would be validated twice under two labels ('verdi.bindings.yaml (<service>)' and 'verdi.bindings.yaml (root)'), duplicating every finding. Harmless for this repository per D6-4, but the guard's own documented intent is defeated by path-normalization asymmetry rather than by semantics. (confidence 0.25) — Path-normalization asymmetry defeats the guard's documented intent for library/test callers (harmless for this repo per D6-4): SCHEDULED for the endgame fix wave — normalize in.Root before comparison.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

(none)
